corrections.text: The article attributes to The Register the quote: "once a service is offered to British users, the entirety of its global turnover is counted." This verbatim phrasing appears in BM Magazine (bmmagazine.co.uk), not The Register. The Register covers the same point but uses different wording ('global turnover rather than UK-specific revenue'). The factual claim is accurate; only the source attribution is incorrect.

The article states Kick Online Entertainment was fined £800,000 for failing to deploy age assurance. The Inforrm source reports a £800,000 fine for the section 12 breach plus a separate £30,000 fine for failure to comply with a statutory information request, making the total fine £830,000.
